skills.name: vendor-concentration
skills.description: Find categories or departments where one vendor controls an unusually high share of spend.
skills.why_it_matters: Concentration is not wrongdoing, but it tells reviewers where competition or procurement design deserves attention.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the vendor's share is high only in a tiny category.
Check whether the same concentration exists across many departments, which may indicate a framework contract rather than a red flag.

skills.name: amendment-creep
skills.description: Find contracts whose current value is materially larger than the original awarded value.
skills.why_it_matters: Large increases after award can point to scope changes, weak initial scoping, or threshold avoidance. They require document review.
disconfirming_checks: Compare the finding's multiple with the peer distribution for the same table.
Check whether the original value field is a placeholder or award ceiling rather than a true original contract value.

skills.name: related-parties
skills.description: Find people appearing across multiple organizations in the loaded datasets.
skills.why_it_matters: Shared names are review leads only. They require identity validation before any governance claim is made.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the person match has an identifier beyond name.
Check whether the connected organizations overlap in time.

skills.name: zombie-recipients
skills.description: Find recipients receiving funding after dissolution, revocation, or last valid filing date.
skills.why_it_matters: A payment after an entity is no longer active is a clean audit lead if status dates are present.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the inactive date postdates the award decision but predates payment for an administrative reason.

skills.name: ghost-capacity
skills.description: Find organizations with high government revenue and low apparent operating capacity.
skills.why_it_matters: Low staff and low program spend can reveal pass-through or shell-like patterns that need review.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the organization is intentionally a grantmaker or pass-through body.

skills.name: funding-loops
skills.description: Find circular transfers among funded organizations.
skills.why_it_matters: Cycles can indicate circular funding, fiscal opacity, or governance issues.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the apparent loop is expected fiscal-agent behavior.

skills.name: policy-misalignment
skills.description: Compare declared policy commitments with actual spending patterns.
skills.why_it_matters: Spending can drift away from stated priorities even when every individual transaction is valid.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the spending category maps to the policy through a different program label.

skills.name: duplicative-funding
skills.description: Find similar grants to the same recipient across overlapping periods or jurisdictions.
skills.why_it_matters: Duplicate or overlapping funding can reveal coordination gaps.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the similar grants fund distinct phases or distinct eligible cost categories.

skills.name: contract-intelligence
skills.description: Decompose spend growth by volume, price, and concentration.
skills.why_it_matters: Rising spend can be benign volume growth or a unit-cost problem. The difference matters.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether growth is explained by volume rather than unit cost.

skills.name: adverse-media
skills.description: Attach cited adverse public-record signals to surfaced entities.
skills.why_it_matters: External context can prioritize review, but it must be cited and treated as unverified until checked.
disconfirming_checks: Require direct source citations and distinguish same-name noise from actual entity matches.

skills.name: geographic-coverage
skills.description: Compare delivery footprint across regions, weighted by population, to find areas that may be underserved.
skills.why_it_matters: Funding can cluster in capital regions even when policy commits to rural or remote access. Coverage gaps are policy stories, not red flags.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the region label is a service catchment rather than a residence region.
Check whether per-capita is the right normalization for this program.

skills.name: program-stability
skills.description: Surface programs or recipients with steady multi-year support and consistent delivery footprint.
skills.why_it_matters: Stable programs with diverse partners and steady metrics are worth documenting and protecting. Success stories matter as much as risk stories.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether stability is just inertia in a program nobody reviewed.
Check whether outcomes data exists or only inputs.

skills.name: delivery-diversity
skills.description: Find programs delivered by many distinct providers across many regions, signaling healthy market structure.
skills.why_it_matters: Diversity of delivery is the inverse of vendor concentration. It tells leaders where competition or community capacity is working.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether providers are formally distinct but related under the same parent.
Check whether diversity is gamed by splitting contracts.

skills.name: scale-readiness
skills.description: Identify small recipients with consistent delivery metrics that could absorb more support without governance risk.
skills.why_it_matters: Underfunded performers are the inverse of capacity risks. Surfacing them lets ministers make growth decisions, not just defensive ones.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the recipient has the governance maturity to scale.
Check whether outcome metrics are self-reported only.

skills.name: commitment-alignment
skills.description: Find programs where stated policy commitments and actual spending move together over time.
skills.why_it_matters: When policy and spend align, the story is that the system worked. That belongs in briefing notes as much as the gaps do.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the spend label maps to the commitment through a different program name.
Check whether the alignment is recent or sustained.

skills.name: tri-jurisdictional-funding
skills.description: Find organizations funded by all three Canadian jurisdictions (CRA charity, federal grants, Alberta) without those funders necessarily knowing about each other.
skills.why_it_matters: Cross-jurisdictional funding can be deliberate cofunding or invisible duplication. Either lens deserves a coordination conversation, not an accusation.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the funding streams support distinct programs, periods, or cost categories.
Check whether the alignment is intentional cofunding under a known program.

skills.name: cra-loop-risk
skills.description: Wrap CRA's pre-computed circular-gifting risk score (0-30) per charity with seven-field story packets.
skills.why_it_matters: CRA's loop_universe scoring already cross-validates self-join and Johnson's algorithm. Our value is to package the high-score charities with caveats and a next action.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the loops are denominational or federated structure rather than gaming.
Check whether the score is driven by one large bottleneck or many small ones.

skills.name: ab-sole-source-concentration
skills.description: Find Alberta ministries where one vendor dominates sole-source spend.
skills.why_it_matters: Sole-source contracts bypass competitive procurement. Concentration there is meaningfully different from concentration in competitive contracts.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the dominance reflects a specialized statutory function (e.g. a single regulator).
Check whether the permitted_situations code justifies the concentration.

skills.name: fed-amendment-creep
skills.description: Find federal agreements whose current value materially exceeds the original (latest amendment compared to is_amendment=false original).
skills.why_it_matters: Implements F-3 mitigation: deduplicates per (ref_number, recipient_key) before comparing. Naive SUM on grants_contributions inflates by ~73%.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the amendments fund distinct phases authorized under the original procurement.
Check whether the original_value is a placeholder rather than a true award amount.

skills.name: cra-shared-directors
skills.description: Directors appearing on the boards of 3+ distinct charities, surfaced as identity-validation review leads.
skills.why_it_matters: T3010 director rosters are the only pan-charity governance signal. Name overlap is a review lead, not a finding; identity validation is required.
disconfirming_checks: Check whether the matched persons share a unique identifier (DOB, address, declared affiliation).
Check whether overlapping board terms actually existed in time.
